title: Just, Yet, Already — Present Perfect vs Past Simple
description: JUST (minutes ago), YET (still expected), ALREADY (sooner than expected). The trio that confuses Brazilians most.
summary_pt_br: JUST / YET / ALREADY são os três sinais que marcam a conexão com o momento presente, típicos do Present Perfect. Diferença crucial: Past Simple tem um tempo específico ('yesterday', 'last week'); Present Perfect se conecta com AGORA.

scenes.text_en:
Maya burst in.
'I HAVE JUST FINISHED the homework!'
Mr. Kumar smiled. 'The rest of you — HAVE you FINISHED YET?'
Joca: 'I HAVE ALREADY FINISHED. Twenty minutes ago.'
Rafa: 'I HAVEN'T STARTED YET. Sorry.'
scenes.text_pt:
A Maya entrou correndo.
'Acabei de terminar a lição!'
Mr. Kumar sorriu. 'O resto — vocês já terminaram?'
Joca: 'Eu já terminei. Vinte minutos atrás.'
Rafa: 'Ainda não comecei. Desculpa.'

scenes.title: Just / Yet / Already — where they live
scenes.body_en: JUST = a moment ago (positive). Usually between have/has and past participle. 'I have JUST arrived.' ALREADY = earlier than expected (positive). Same position. 'She has ALREADY left.' YET = up to now (negative/question). At the END of the sentence. 'Have you finished YET?' 'I haven't eaten YET.'
scenes.body_pt: JUST = acabou de (positiva). Entre have/has e particípio. ALREADY = já (positiva, mais cedo que esperado). Mesma posição. YET = ainda (pergunta/negativa). No FIM da frase. 'Haven't finished YET'.

scenes.title: Present Perfect vs Past Simple — the TIME rule
scenes.body_en: If the sentence has a SPECIFIC past time (yesterday, last week, in 2020, at 3 p.m.), use Past Simple. 'I ATE breakfast at 7.' If NO specific time (and it connects to now), use Present Perfect. 'I HAVE EATEN breakfast.' Brazilians mix these — the rule is about the TIME SIGNAL, not the meaning.
scenes.body_pt: Com tempo ESPECÍFICO (yesterday, last week, in 2020): use Past Simple. Sem tempo específico (ligado ao agora): Present Perfect. O erro clássico brasileiro é misturar — a regra é sobre o MARCADOR DE TEMPO, não o sentido.

examples.en: I saw her yesterday. (specific → past simple)
I have seen her three times. (no specific → present perfect)

exercise.question: Which is correct?
exercise.explanation: YET goes at the END of questions and negatives.
exercise.hint_pt_br: YET no fim da frase, sempre.

exercise.question: Complete: 'She has ___ arrived — literally two minutes ago.'
exercise.explanation: JUST = very recently. Between 'has' and the participle.
exercise.hint_pt_br: 'Just' = acabou de. Vem entre 'has' e o particípio.

exercise.question: Which uses the WRONG tense?
options: I have seen her three times
I saw her three times last year
exercise.explanation: 'Yesterday' is a specific past time → Past Simple (saw), NOT Present Perfect.
exercise.hint_pt_br: 'Yesterday' é tempo específico → Past Simple. Nunca 'have seen yesterday'.

exercise.explanation: Five signals. Master where each one sits in the sentence.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Just, Yet, Already — Present Perfect vs Past Simple". JUST (minutes ago), YET (still expected), ALREADY (sooner than expected). The trio that confuses Brazilians most. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave é a prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus próprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Just, Yet, Already — Present Perfect vs Past Simple". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule about the past
Just, Yet, Already — Present Perfect vs Past Simple
check.explanation: She says: "Today in class we studied 'Just, Yet, Already — Present Perfect vs Past Simple'".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala claramente o título: "Just, Yet, Already — Present Perfect vs Past Simple".

scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para você estudar mais. Salve os links e volte sempre.
